{{Quote|A hot air balloon! We have everything we need. We can make the parachute into a balloon. And we have the stove, and fuel.|Stuart Zissu|<ref name="X" />}}
A '''hot air balloon''', or simply '''balloon''' was an ancient type of aircraft that ran on hot air. It consisted of a large bag of light material that was filled with heated gas to make it rise in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. Such balloons often carried a suspended "gondola" for passengers.

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|The burner heats the air inside the envelope. Heated air is less dense than the cooler air around it. So it rises—or floats upwards.|4B-X|<ref name="X" />}}
[[File:HotAirBalloon.jpg|thumb|left|Schematics for a basic hot air balloon]]
The scientific fact that hot air rose above cold air could be used to put a balloon into motion and make it leave the ground. A hot air balloon consisted of three basic parts: the basket or "gondola" for the passengers to sit in, the large bag known as the "envelope" that would fill with air and lift the basket, and the burner which was used to heat the air in the envelope.<ref name="X" />

Unlike more modern aircrafts, hot air balloons had no steering controls, which could be very destabilizing to individuals hailing from [[Technology level|advanced]] civilizations. The burner was the closest equivalent to controls, allowing the pilot to take the balloon up or down by adjusting the intensity of heat. In order to make the balloon move laterally, its pilot needed to know the layers of wind currents so he or she could determine which one would push the aircraft in the desired direction.<ref name="X" />

On the world of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]] used [[Dunsenn FlopCarrier|troop carriers]] that functioned on similar principles, except that the gas-filled bag was replaced by a [[dunsenn]], a lighter-than-air, [[Fish/Legends|fish]]-like animal of vast size.<ref name="SWGB">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref>

==History==
{{Quote|Luke said the Lahsbees' dislike of complicated machinery is what made them friendlier toward the Rebellion than the Empire... But I wish they went in for something a little more sophisticated—and safer—than these balloons.|Leia Organa|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 73">[[Star Wars (1977) 73|''Star Wars'' (1977) 73]]</ref>}}
They were notably used on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Lahsbane/Legends|Lahsbane]] to cross the broad canyons that existed there. The native [[Lahsbee/Huhk]] aversion to technology meant this was one of the most sophisticated forms of air transport on the planet. [[Glider (vehicle)/Legends|Gliders]] were also used to cross the canyons.<ref name="M73">[[Star Wars (1977) 73|''Star Wars'' (1977) 73]]</ref>

A makeshift hot air balloon—made from a parachute, a piece of hull from an [[Escape pod 4|escape pod]] and a homemade stove—was used by [[Stuart Zissu]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[4B-X]] on [[Da'nor]] to return to civilization. It was destroyed when they were forced to land during a storm and crashed through a canopy of trees.<ref name="X" />

{{Unlicensedstart}}
At some point in history, a [[Unidentified traveling explorer|traveling explorer]] crashed with their balloon on the top of the [[Explorer's Crag]], on the forested [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. The native [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] later found that balloon and used it for their own enjoyment.<ref name="TB">{{StoryCite|story=The Balloon|book=MyComyc|bformatted=''MyComyc'' 5}}</ref>
{{Unlicensedend}}
